# Now Playing API for Web Radios

> Documentation-only repository for the hosted Now Playing API service. The public endpoint is available at https://api.twj.es/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fstream.zeno.fm%2Fyn65fsaurfhvv and returns now-playing metadata for web radios. This repository does not publish the executable implementation.

## Docs
- [README](https://github.com/jailsonsb2/now-playing-api#readme): endpoint, response format, and usage examples
- [Docs site](https://jailsonsb2.github.io/now-playing-api/): public landing page and live tester

## Consumption
- Endpoint: https://api.twj.es/?url=STREAM_URL
- Response fields: songtitle, artist, song, album, albumArt, streamUrl, youtubeId, now_playing, song_history

## Service behavior (important for integrations)
- First request for a radio returns "Carregando..." with "loading": true — it is a state, not a song (do not look up cover art or lyrics for it); keep polling, real data arrives within seconds.
- Enrichment is asynchronous: albumArt and youtubeId may appear one or two polls later; compare songtitle AND enrichment fields to detect updates.
- ICY is law: artist/song are always what the radio broadcast — enrichment never renames the track.
- Responses have a 5s shared cache and support ETag/If-None-Match; poll every 10 seconds per listener.
- youtubeId powers a "clip mode" (music video of the current song, synchronized via now_playing.elapsed as the embed start); each song_history item also carries youtubeId for clickable history.
- Radios with no listeners for 5 minutes stop being monitored and resume automatically on the next request.

## Free players that use this API
- https://github.com/jailsonsb2/RadioPlayer — full-page player with clip mode and clickable history
- https://github.com/jailsonsb2/Radioplayer_api — multi-station, 3 layouts
- https://github.com/jailsonsb2/bottom_radioplayer — bottom-bar component, audio never stops on navigation
- https://github.com/jailsonsb2/RadioPlayer-ZenoRadio — full-page player for Zeno.FM

## Notes
- This repository is for documentation, visibility, and education.
- The service is consumed through the hosted endpoint rather than through published source code.
